# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1 `[KTU University Exam — May 2024]`
**Define "emergence" in the context of large-scale self-organizing intelligent agent systems. State any two distinguishing properties.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
Emergence is the phenomenon in which coherent, system-level intelligent behaviour arises from the local interactions of many autonomous agents, without any agent possessing a global blueprint or central controller. *Two distinguishing properties:* **(1)** The global pattern is **not explicitly represented** in any single agent; **(2)** The pattern is **robust** to the failure or removal of individual agents (graceful degradation). *(1 mark for definition, 1 mark each for the two properties = 3 marks)*

### Question 2 `[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023]`
**What is stigmergy? How is it useful in a chemical plant inspection swarm?**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
Stigmergy is a mechanism of indirect coordination between agents in which the actions of one agent modify the environment, which in turn influences the future actions of other agents. In a chemical-plant inspection swarm, drones deposit a "pheromone" (a numerical weight) on the digital map wherever they detect a hotspot (e.g., a high-temperature spot on a reactor shell). Other drones preferentially follow the gradient of this pheromone field, so the swarm *self-organizes* to concentrate inspection effort on the most critical zones — without any central dispatcher. *(1 mark definition, 1 mark chemical-plant context, 1 mark self-organization benefit = 3 marks)*

---

## 5.2 Part B — Full-Length Questions (14 Marks Each, Internal Choice)

### Question A `[KTU University Exam — Module 4 Model Paper]`
**(a) [7 marks]** With a neat block diagram, describe the architecture of a self-organizing multi-agent system deployed for plant-wide process control in a refinery. Clearly label the **physical layer**, **agent layer**, **holon layer**, and **plant-wide intelligence layer**.

**(b) [7 marks]** Starting from the generic agent update rule $x_i(t+1) = f(x_i(t), \{x_j(t)\}_{j \in \mathcal{N}_i})$, derive the **continuous-time consensus equation** for $N$ agents connected through a graph with Laplacian $L$. Show that the system converges to a common value equal to the initial average $\bar{x}(0)$ provided the graph is connected.

### Question B `[KTU University Exam — Module 4 Model Paper]` (Internal Choice)
**(a) [7 marks]** Define the **order parameter** $\mathcal{O}$ for a swarm of $N$ self-organizing agents. Explain how $\mathcal{O}$ quantifies the degree of emergence. Sketch (describe) the phase-transition behaviour of $\mathcal{O}$ versus the noise strength $\eta$ and identify the critical noise $\eta_c$.

**(b) [7 marks]** For an inspection-drone swarm, the stigmergic pheromone update is given by $s_{ij}(t+1) = (1-\rho)\, s_{ij}(t) + \mu (T_{ij} - T_{threshold})$. **(i)** Derive the steady-state pheromone level $s_{ij}^{ss}$. **(ii)** If $\rho = 0.05$, $\mu = 0.02$, $T_{ij} = 425$ K, and $T_{threshold} = 380$ K, compute $s_{ij}^{ss}$. **(iii)** Comment on how doubling the evaporation rate $\rho$ would affect $s_{ij}^{ss}$.

### Model Solutions for Question A

**Part (a) Solution — 7 marks:**

The four-layer architecture (refer to *Section 4.1* diagram) is described as follows:
1. **Physical Layer (1 mark):** Contains the actual process equipment — reactors, distillation columns, valves, sensors, actuators.
2. **Agent Layer (2 marks):** Each piece of equipment is wrapped by a software agent. Each agent runs the **BDI (Belief–Desire–Intention)** cycle with a cycle time of $\Delta t \in [0.1, 1.0]$ s. Agents have direct read/write access only to their own equipment.
3. **Holon Layer (2 marks):** Groups of 3–6 agents form a *holon* (a stable sub-swarm) following the **ISA-88 / ISA-95** holonic manufacturing standard. A holon coordinator resolves intra-holon conflicts.
4. **Plant-wide Intelligence Layer (2 marks):** Emergent plant-level behaviour arises from peer-to-peer communication between holons via **FIPA-ACL** messages. No single layer-4 controller exists; intelligence is *distributed* and *emergent*.

**Part (b) Solution — 7 marks:**

**Step 1 (1 mark):** Local rule — agent $i$ drifts toward the average of its neighbours' states:
$$\dot{x}_i = k\!\left( \frac{1}{d_i}\sum_{j=1}^{N} a_{ij} x_j - x_i \right), \quad k > 0$$

**Step 2 (1 mark):** Rearranging:
$$\dot{x}_i = \frac{k}{d_i}\sum_{j=1}^{N} a_{ij}\,(x_j - x_i)$$

**Step 3 (1 mark):** In vector form, $X = [x_1, \dots, x_N]^{\top}$:
$$\dot{X} = -k\, D^{-1} L\, X$$

For a *degree-regular* graph, $D = d\,I$ and:
$$\dot{X} = -\frac{k}{d}\, L\, X$$

**Step 4 (1 mark):** Since $L$ is symmetric, diagonalize $L = V \Lambda V^{\top}$ with $V$ orthogonal and $\Lambda = \mathrm{diag}(0, \lambda_2, \dots, \lambda_N)$. The solution is:
$$X(t) = V e^{-k D^{-1} \Lambda t} V^{\top} X(0)$$

**Step 5 (1 mark):** All modes with $\lambda_i > 0$ decay exponentially. The $\lambda_1 = 0$ mode has eigenvector $v_1 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{N}}\mathbf{1}$.

**Step 6 (1 mark):** As $t \to \infty$:
$$\lim_{t \to \infty} X(t) = \left(\frac{\mathbf{1}^{\top} X(0)}{N}\right)\mathbf{1} = \bar{x}(0)\,\mathbf{1}$$

**Step 7 (1 mark):** *Connectivity condition:* $\lambda_2 > 0$ if and only if the graph is connected. Hence, a connected graph guarantees consensus.

### Model Solutions for Question B

**Part (a) Solution — 7 marks:**

**Definition (2 marks):** For agents with 2-D unit orientation vectors $\vec{s}_i$:
$$\mathcal{O} = \frac{\left\Vert \sum_{i=1}^{N} \vec{s}_i \right\Vert}{N}, \quad \mathcal{O} \in [0, 1]$$

**Interpretation (2 marks):** $\mathcal{O} \approx 0$ ⇒ disordered swarm (no emergence); $\mathcal{O} \approx 1$ ⇒ fully ordered swarm (maximum emergence). It is a *macroscopic* scalar summarizing a *microscopic* multi-agent state.

**Phase transition (3 marks):** As noise $\eta$ increases from 0, $\mathcal{O}$ remains near 1 up to a critical noise $\eta_c$, then drops sharply to 0:
$$\mathcal{O} \approx \begin{cases} \left(\dfrac{\eta_c}{\eta}\right)^{\beta}, & \eta < \eta_c \\[6pt] 0, & \eta \ge \eta_c \end{cases}, \quad \beta \approx 0.45$$
This is a **second-order phase transition** analogous to ferromagnetic Curie transition. In a chemical plant, $\eta$ maps to **sensor noise + communication delay + actuator jitter**.

**Part (b) Solution — 7 marks:**

**(i) Derivation (2 marks):** Setting $s_{ij}(t+1) = s_{ij}(t) = s_{ij}^{ss}$:
$$s_{ij}^{ss} = (1-\rho)\, s_{ij}^{ss} + \mu(T_{ij} - T_{threshold})$$
$$\rho\, s_{ij}^{ss} = \mu(T_{ij} - T_{threshold})$$
$$s_{ij}^{ss} = \frac{\mu(T_{ij} - T_{threshold})}{\rho}$$

**(ii) Numerical computation (3 marks):**
- Numerator: $\mu (T_{ij} - T_{threshold}) = 0.02 \times (425 - 380) = 0.02 \times 45 = 0.90$
- Denominator: $\rho = 0.05$
- $s_{ij}^{ss} = 0.90 / 0.05 = 18.0$ (dimensionless pheromone units)

**(iii) Comment (2 marks):** Doubling $\rho$ to $0.10$ would *halve* $s_{ij}^{ss}$ to $9.0$ (since $s_{ij}^{ss} \propto 1/\rho$). The pheromone "memory" is shorter, the swarm becomes more reactive to *new* hotspots but loses persistence on *old* ones. A control engineer must therefore tune $\rho$ to balance *exploration* (low $\rho$) vs. *exploitation* (high $\rho$).

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall — Common Reasons for Losing Marks**
> 1. **Confusing $L$ and $D$:** In the consensus derivation, students often write $L = A - D$ instead of $L = D - A$. This produces the *opposite* sign in the dynamics and breaks the Lyapunov proof. *Always remember: **L = D − A***. **(−2 marks)**
> 2. **Forgetting the connectivity condition:** Stating consensus convergence without specifying that the graph must be **connected** ($\lambda_2 > 0$) loses 1 full mark.
> 3. **Mixing absolute-value pipes with table pipes:** Writing $\vert x \vert$ using a plain `|` character inside a markdown table **breaks the table renderer**. Always use $\vert x \vert$ inside a math environment.
> 4. **Skipping the Lyapunov step:** Many students skip showing that $V(x) = x^{\top} L x \ge 0$ and $\dot{V} \le 0$. This is the rigorous proof of stability — *always* include it for full marks.
> 5. **Confusing stigmergy with direct communication:** Stigmergy is *indirect* (via environment), not message-passing. **(−1 mark)**

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid-Revision Checklist)

- [x] **Self-organizing agent = autonomous + reactive + pro-active + social.** (BDI model)
- [x] **Emergence = global order from local rules, no central blueprint.**
- [x] **Stigmergy = indirect coordination via environment** (pheromone trails).
- [x] **Laplacian $L = D - A$ is symmetric, positive semi-definite, with one zero eigenvalue for connected graph.**
- [x] **Continuous consensus:** $\dot{x}_i = -\sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}_i} a_{ij}(x_i - x_j)$.
- [x] **Convergence value = initial global average** $\bar{x}(0)$ — this is the emergent agreement.
- [x] **Connectivity is necessary** — $\lambda_2(L) > 0$ is the algebraic connectivity (Fiedler value).
- [x] **Order parameter** $\mathcal{O} = \Vert \sum \vec{s}_i \Vert / N$ quantifies emergence; phase transition at $\eta_c$.
- [x] **Pheromone steady state:** $s_{ij}^{ss} = \mu(T_{ij} - T_{threshold}) / \rho$.
- [x] **Four-layer MAS architecture:** Physical → Agent → Holon → Plant-wide.
- [x] **Industry 4.0 / 5.0 mapping:** Multi-agent systems replace centralized DCS.
- [x] **Key KTU CO tags:** CO4 (Analyze) + CO5 (Evaluate) — frequently tested as 14-mark questions.
- [x] **Avoid common pipe character in tables** — use $\vert$ or $\Vert$ in math mode.
- [x] **Mention the Lyapunov function** $V = x^{\top} L x$ in any consensus proof for full marks.
